definition.en: A single knowledge base â€” a collection of Sources, compiled Neurons, and curator decisions held together by a shared topic or project. A user can have many Trails ("my zoneterapi Trail", "my iOS-dev notes Trail").
definition.da: En enkelt vidensbase â€” en samling af Kilder, kompilerede Neuroner og kurator-beslutninger holdt sammen af et fĂ¦lles emne eller projekt. En bruger kan have mange Trails ("min zoneterapi-Trail", "min iOS-udviklings-Trail").

definition.en: A compiled page inside a Trail â€” a short, claim-dense markdown document generated from one or more Sources. Neurons link to each other with [[Neuron-links]] and cite Sources via frontmatter.
definition.da: En kompileret side i en Trail â€” et kort, pĂĄstands-tĂ¦t markdown-dokument genereret fra en eller flere Kilder. Neuroner linker til hinanden med [[Neuron-links]] og citerer Kilder via frontmatter.

definition.en: An immutable raw document uploaded into a Trail â€” PDF, DOCX, MD, plain text. Sources are never edited after ingest; Neurons are compiled FROM them and cite them by filename.
definition.da: Et uforanderligt rĂĄt dokument uploadet til en Trail â€” PDF, DOCX, MD, almindelig tekst. Kilder bliver aldrig redigeret efter ingest; Neuroner kompileres FRA dem og citerer dem ved filnavn.

definition.en: The human who decides what lives in a Trail. Curators review candidates in the Queue, approve or reject them, and occasionally edit Neurons directly. The Curator is the source of truth â€” the LLM proposes, the Curator disposes.
definition.da: Det menneske der bestemmer hvad der lever i en Trail. Kuratorer gennemgĂĄr kandidater i KĂ¸en, godkender eller afviser dem, og redigerer af og til Neuroner direkte. Kuratoren er den endelige autoritet â€” LLM'en foreslĂĄr, Kuratoren beslutter.

definition.en: The list of pending changes waiting for the Curator's decision. Every write into a Trail (new Neuron, edit, archive, lint alert) lands here first; the Queue is the ONLY path that can modify Neurons.
definition.da: Listen af afventende Ă¦ndringer der venter pĂĄ Kuratorens beslutning. Alle skrivninger i en Trail (ny Neuron, redigering, arkivering, lint-advarsel) lander her fĂ¸rst; KĂ¸en er den ENESTE vej der kan Ă¦ndre Neuroner.

definition.en: A single proposed change to a Trail â€” a new Neuron, an edit, an archive, or an alert from Lint. Each Candidate has a status (pending, approved, rejected) and a set of Actions the Curator can choose from.
definition.da: En enkelt foreslĂĄet Ă¦ndring til en Trail â€” en ny Neuron, en redigering, en arkivering eller en advarsel fra Lint. Hver Kandidat har en status (afventer, godkendt, afvist) og et sĂ¦t Handlinger som Kuratoren kan vĂ¦lge imellem.

definition.en: One of the resolution options on a Candidate. Default Candidates have Approve + Reject; richer Candidates (contradiction, orphan, stale) carry producer-specific Actions like "Retire Neuron", "Link to sources", "Dismiss as false positive". Each Action has a plain-language explanation describing what happens when you click it.
definition.da: En af beslutnings-mulighederne pĂĄ en Kandidat. Standard-kandidater har Godkend + Afvis; rigere Kandidater (modsigelse, forĂ¦ldrelĂ¸s, forĂ¦ldet) bĂ¦rer producer-specifikke Handlinger som "Arkiver Neuron", "Link til kilder", "Afvis som falsk positiv". Hver Handling har en lĂ¦gmand-forklaring der beskriver hvad der sker nĂĄr du klikker pĂĄ den.

definition.en: Background scan of a Trail for structural problems â€” orphan Neurons (no citations), stale Neurons (untouched too long), contradictions (two Neurons with conflicting claims). Findings land as Candidates in the Queue. Runs on a schedule (nightly) and reactively on curator decisions.
definition.da: Baggrunds-scanning af en Trail for strukturelle problemer â€” forĂ¦ldrelĂ¸se Neuroner (ingen citationer), forĂ¦ldede Neuroner (urĂ¸rt for lĂ¦nge), modsigelser (to Neuroner med modstridende pĂĄstande). Fund lander som Kandidater i KĂ¸en. KĂ¸rer pĂĄ tidsplan (hver nat) og reaktivt pĂĄ kurator-beslutninger.

definition.en: A Neuron that cites no Sources in its frontmatter. Either the compiler wrote it without linking evidence, or the citation was lost in an edit. Lint flags it so the Curator can link the claims to Sources or archive the Neuron if its claims can't be defended.
definition.da: En Neuron der ikke citerer nogen Kilder i sit frontmatter. Enten skrev kompileren den uden at linke til evidens, eller ogsĂĄ er citationen gĂĄet tabt i en redigering. Lint markerer det sĂĄ Kuratoren kan linke pĂĄstandene til Kilder eller arkivere Neuronen hvis pĂĄstandene ikke kan forsvares.

definition.en: A Neuron that hasn't been touched in a long time (default 90 days). Not automatically wrong, but the most likely to have drifted from what its Sources now say. Lint flags it so the Curator can confirm it's still accurate, refresh it from the Source, or archive.
definition.da: En Neuron der ikke har vĂ¦ret rĂ¸rt i lang tid (standard 90 dage). Ikke automatisk forkert, men den der har stĂ¸rst chance for at vĂ¦re drevet vĂ¦k fra hvad Kilderne siger nu. Lint markerer den sĂĄ Kuratoren kan bekrĂ¦fte den stadig er prĂ¦cis, opfriske den fra Kilden eller arkivere.

definition.en: Two Neurons whose claims cannot both be true under standard reading. Detected by pairwise LLM comparison after a new or updated Neuron is approved. The Curator decides which side is right: retire one, reconcile manually, flag the Source, or dismiss as a false positive.
definition.da: To Neuroner hvis pĂĄstande ikke begge kan vĂ¦re sande under almindelig lĂ¦sning. Opdaget af parvis LLM-sammenligning efter en ny eller opdateret Neuron er godkendt. Kuratoren beslutter hvilken side der har ret: arkiver den ene, forlig manuelt, markĂ©r Kilden, eller afvis som falsk positiv.

definition.en: A Lint finding that isn't actually a problem. The detector got it wrong â€” different phrasing looked like a contradiction, an intentionally-axiomatic page looked like an orphan, an evergreen reference looked stale. Dismissing as false positive blocks the finding from re-surfacing (in Trusting policy mode).
definition.da: Et Lint-fund der ikke reelt er et problem. Detektoren tog fejl â€” forskellig formulering lignede en modsigelse, en bevidst aksiomatisk side lignede en forĂ¦ldrelĂ¸s, en evigtgyldig reference virkede forĂ¦ldet. At afvise som falsk positiv blokerer fundet fra at dukke op igen (i Tillidsfuld policy-tilstand).

definition.en: Per-Trail setting controlling what happens to rejected Lint findings. "Trusting" (default) permanently suppresses them â€” no nag-loop. "Strict" lets them re-fire on the next Lint pass for a second review. Versioned fingerprints re-qualify findings after the Neuron has actually been edited, independent of policy.
definition.da: Per-Trail indstilling der styrer hvad der sker med afviste Lint-fund. "Tillidsfuld" (standard) undertrykker dem permanent â€” ingen nag-lĂ¸kke. "Streng" lader dem dukke op igen pĂĄ nĂ¦ste Lint-pass til en anden gennemgang. Versionerede fingeraftryk kvalificerer fund igen nĂĄr Neuronen faktisk er blevet redigeret, uanset politik.

definition.en: A stable identifier for a Lint finding, used to dedupe re-runs. A nightly Lint pass that finds the same orphan Neuron as yesterday skips re-emitting it. Includes the Neuron's version so a substantive rewrite creates a fresh fingerprint that bypasses old suppressions.
definition.da: En stabil identifikator for et Lint-fund, brugt til at fjerne dubletter ved gen-kĂ¸rsler. Et nattetids Lint-pass der finder samme forĂ¦ldrelĂ¸se Neuron som i gĂĄr springer re-emission over. Inkluderer Neuronens version, sĂĄ en substantiel omskrivning skaber et nyt fingeraftryk der omgĂĄr gamle undertrykkelser.

definition.en: Policy that approves a Candidate without curator review. Fires for trusted pipelines (e.g. ingest-summary) and for candidates above a confidence threshold. Human-originated candidates never auto-approve by design â€” people click "submit", machines emit confidences.
definition.da: Politik der godkender en Kandidat uden kurator-gennemgang. UdlĂ¸ses for betroede pipelines (fx ingest-resumĂ©) og for kandidater over en sikkerhedsgrĂ¦nse. Menneske-initierede kandidater auto-godkendes aldrig efter design â€” mennesker klikker "indsend", maskiner udsender sikkerheder.

definition.en: The LLM step that turns extracted Source text into one or more Neurons. Takes the chunked body plus a compile prompt (see `apps/server/src/services/ingest.ts`), the Trail's language, its existing tag list, and the set of entity Neurons it already holds (F148), then asks the model to emit claim-dense markdown pages â€” each with YAML frontmatter (`title`, `sources: [filename.pdf]`, `tags: [...]`, optional `type`/`kind` per F101) and `[[Neuron-links]]` to related pages. The output lands as Queue candidates (`ingest-summary` for new, `ingest-page-update` for edits); auto-approval policy decides whether they commit without a human click. Compile is what F149's pluggable ingest backends parameterise â€” same input, different model: Claude CLI via `spawnClaude` (Max Plan default), or OpenRouter for Gemini Flash, GLM, Qwen, direct Claude API. A run emits an F136 compile-log card with turn count, cost, and wall-clock. **Distinct from:** *Ingest* (the full pipeline wrapping compile with extraction + chunking + candidate emission), *Retry* (re-runs everything including file-format extraction), *Re-ingest* (re-runs only compile against already-extracted content).
definition.da: LLM-trinnet der forvandler udtrukket Kilde-tekst til Ă©n eller flere Neuroner. Tager det opdelte indhold plus en kompileringsprompt (se `apps/server/src/services/ingest.ts`), Trail'ens sprog, dens eksisterende tag-liste og sĂ¦ttet af entity-Neuroner den allerede har (F148), og beder modellen udsende pĂĄstands-tĂ¦tte markdown-sider â€” hver med YAML-frontmatter (`title`, `sources: [filnavn.pdf]`, `tags: [...]`, valgfri `type`/`kind` jf. F101) og `[[Neuron-links]]` til relaterede sider. Output'et lander som KĂ¸-kandidater (`ingest-summary` for nye, `ingest-page-update` for redigeringer); auto-godkendelsespolitikken afgĂ¸r om de committes uden menneskeklik. Kompilering er det F149's pluggbare ingest-backends parameteriserer â€” samme input, forskellig model: Claude CLI via `spawnClaude` (Max Plan-standard), eller OpenRouter for Gemini Flash, GLM, Qwen, direkte Claude API. En kĂ¸rsel udsender et F136 kompileringslog-kort med turn-antal, omkostning og wall-clock. **Adskilt fra:** *Ingest* (hele pipelinen der pakker compile ind sammen med udtrĂ¦kning + opdeling + kandidat-udsendelse), *PrĂ¸v igen* (kĂ¸rer alt om inkl. filformat-udtrĂ¦kning), *Re-ingest* (kĂ¸rer kun kompilering igen mod allerede udtrukket indhold).

definition.en: The process of reading a new Source, extracting its text, chunking it for search, and compiling one or more Neurons from it. Ingest produces ingest-summary candidates that auto-approve if confidence is high enough.
definition.da: Processen med at lĂ¦se en ny Kilde, udtrĂ¦kke dens tekst, opdele den til sĂ¸gning og kompilere en eller flere Neuroner fra den. Ingest producerer ingest-resumĂ©-kandidater der auto-godkendes hvis sikkerheden er hĂ¸j nok.

definition.en: Re-run the ENTIRE pipeline for a Source â€” re-extract the file (PDF text, DOCX HTML, PPTX slides, XLSX cells), re-chunk, and re-ingest. Use this when extraction itself failed (corrupt PDF, silent pdfjs hang, vision-API hiccup) or when you've replaced the underlying file bytes. More expensive than re-ingest because extraction is re-done; prefer re-ingest when the extracted content is fine and only the LLM compile needs another pass.
definition.da: KĂ¸r HELE pipelinen igen for en Kilde â€” genudtrĂ¦k filen (PDF-tekst, DOCX-HTML, PPTX-slides, XLSX-celler), genopdel, og re-ingest. Brug dette nĂĄr selve udtrĂ¦kningen fejlede (korrupt PDF, tavs pdfjs-hang, vision-API-hikke) eller nĂĄr du har udskiftet de underliggende fil-bytes. Dyrere end re-ingest fordi udtrĂ¦kning kĂ¸res igen; foretrĂ¦k re-ingest nĂĄr det udtrukne indhold er fint og kun LLM-kompileringen skal have endnu et forsĂ¸g.

definition.en: Re-run ONLY the LLM compile step against a Source's already-extracted content. Skips file-format extraction (no PDF re-parse, no vision re-run) and goes straight to spawning the Claude compile subprocess. Cheap and fast â€” useful when ingest hit a budget ceiling (timeout, max-turns), when you've raised TRAIL_INGEST_MAX_TURNS, or when you've updated the compile prompt and want existing Sources to benefit. If extraction itself failed, use Retry instead.
definition.da: KĂ¸r KUN LLM-kompilerings-trinnet igen mod en Kildes allerede-udtrukne indhold. Springer over filformat-udtrĂ¦kning (ingen PDF-genparsing, ingen vision-genkĂ¸rsel) og gĂĄr direkte til at starte Claude-kompilerings-subprocessen. Billigt og hurtigt â€” nyttigt nĂĄr ingest ramte et budgetloft (timeout, max-turns), nĂĄr du har hĂ¦vet TRAIL_INGEST_MAX_TURNS, eller nĂĄr du har opdateret kompilerings-prompten og vil have eksisterende Kilder til at drage fordel af det. Hvis selve udtrĂ¦kningen fejlede, brug PrĂ¸v igen i stedet.

definition.en: A tracked citation from a Neuron to a Source (provenance â€” "this claim came from this document") OR a [[Neuron-link]] from a Neuron to another Neuron (navigation â€” "see also"). Two separate graphs; the Curator sees both as one interconnected whole.
definition.da: En sporet citation fra en Neuron til en Kilde (herkomst â€” "denne pĂĄstand kom fra dette dokument") ELLER et [[Neuron-link]] fra en Neuron til en anden Neuron (navigation â€” "se ogsĂĄ"). To separate grafer; Kuratoren ser begge som Ă©n sammenhĂ¦ngende helhed.

definition.en: The YAML block at the top of every Neuron, between two `---` lines. Holds structured metadata: title, tags, date, and `sources: [...]` â€” the filenames the Neuron's claims came from. The reference extractor reads `sources` on save and writes the document_references rows that the backlink graph + orphan-detector rely on. Missing `sources` â†’ Neuron shows up as an orphan on next Lint run.
definition.da:
YAML-blokken Ă¸verst i hver Neuron, mellem to `---`-linjer. Holder struktureret metadata: title, tags, date, og `sources: [...]` â€” filnavnene pĂĄ de Kilder Neuronens pĂĄstande stammer fra. Reference-ekstraktoren lĂ¦ser `sources` ved gemning og skriver document_references-rĂ¦kkerne som baglink-grafen og forĂ¦ldrelĂ¸s-detektoren bygger pĂĄ. Manglende `sources` â†’ Neuronen dukker op som forĂ¦ldrelĂ¸s ved nĂ¦ste Lint-kĂ¸rsel.

Dansk har intet etableret ord for dette; "frontmatter" bruges som lĂĄneord i dansk Jekyll/Hugo/static-site-tradition. Trail fĂ¸lger samme konvention.

definition.en: The `[[...]]` double-bracket syntax that links one Neuron to another inside a Trail. The text between the brackets is slugified to resolve against a target filename â€” `[[Orphans + Stale]]` finds `orphans-stale.md`. Neuron-links build the graph that powers the Chat's context-gathering, the Backlink view in the reader, and the orphan-detector's "no one links here" signal. **How to author one:** type `[[Target Neuron Title]]` directly in any markdown content field â€” the Neuron editor, the "+ New" modal, a Work item's Description (Task / Bug / Milestone / Decision), or the save-dialog when you pin a Chat answer as a Neuron. The backlink extractor resolves it on save; the new edge appears in the Graph and the target Neuron's "N Neurons link here" count ticks up. The ingest pipeline also writes Neuron-links automatically when compiling Sources, so the Curator rarely has to add them by hand. Optional typed form `[[target|edge-type]]` (F137) carries a semantic relation â€” `cites` (default, bare mention), `is-a`, `part-of`, `contradicts`, `supersedes`, `example-of`, `caused-by` â€” and colours the graph accordingly. Subject to link-integrity guarantees (F148): an unresolvable link becomes a broken-link finding for the Curator, never a 404 on the reader.
definition.da: Den `[[...]]`-dobbeltparentes-syntaks der linker Ă©n Neuron til en anden inden for en Trail. Teksten mellem parenteserne slugifiseres og slĂĄs op mod et mĂĄl-filnavn â€” `[[Yin og Yang]]` finder `yin-og-yang.md`. Neuron-links opbygger grafen der driver Chat'ens konteksthĂ¸st, Baglink-visningen i lĂ¦seren og forĂ¦ldrelĂ¸s-detektorens "ingen linker hertil"-signal. **SĂĄdan laver du selv et:** skriv `[[Titel pĂĄ mĂĄl-Neuron]]` direkte i et markdown-indholdsfelt â€” Neuron-editoren, "+ NYT"-modalet, et Work-items Beskrivelse-felt (Opgave / Fejl / MilepĂ¦l / Beslutning), eller gem-dialogen nĂĄr du pinner et Chat-svar som en Neuron. Baglink-extractoren oplĂ¸ser det ved gemning; den nye kant dukker op i Grafen og mĂĄl-Neuronens "N Neuroner linker hertil"-tĂ¦ller stiger. Ingest-pipelinen skriver ogsĂĄ Neuron-links automatisk nĂĄr den kompilerer Kilder, sĂĄ Kuratoren sjĂ¦ldent skal tilfĂ¸je dem manuelt. Valgfri typet form `[[mĂĄl|edge-type]]` (F137) bĂ¦rer en semantisk relation â€” `cites` (standard, simpel omtale), `is-a`, `part-of`, `contradicts`, `supersedes`, `example-of`, `caused-by` â€” og farver grafen derefter. Underlagt link-integritet-garantien (F148): et ikke-oplĂ¸seligt link bliver til en broken-link-observation til Kuratoren, aldrig en 404 pĂĄ lĂ¦seren.

definition.en: A URL- and filename-safe version of a title, produced by a deterministic transformation called *slugification*: lowercase everything, replace spaces with hyphens, strip punctuation, and fold non-ASCII characters to their closest ASCII equivalents (`Ă¦â†’ae`, `Ă¸â†’oe`, `ĂĄâ†’aa`). Examples: `"Yin og Yang"` â†’ `yin-og-yang`, `"Orphans + Stale"` â†’ `orphans-stale`, `"Christian Broberg"` â†’ `christian-broberg`. Slugs are the canonical address of a Neuron inside a Trail â€” the file is `<slug>.md`, the URL is `/kb/<kb-slug>/neurons/<slug>`, and a Neuron-link's `[[...]]` text gets slugified to find its target. Two different titles that slugify to the same string collide on the same Neuron, so the compiler avoids ambiguity by keeping titles distinctive in the source language. The Danish/English bilingual fold (F148, `normalizedSlug`) recognises that `yin-og-yang` and `yin-and-yang` should resolve to the same Neuron when the KB's language is Danish â€” protecting against LLM drift between `og` / `and`.
definition.da: En URL- og filnavn-sikker version af en titel, lavet af en deterministisk transformation kaldt *slugificering*: alt til smĂĄ bogstaver, mellemrum bliver til bindestreger, tegnsĂ¦tning fjernes, og ikke-ASCII-tegn foldes til nĂ¦rmeste ASCII-Ă¦kvivalent (`Ă¦â†’ae`, `Ă¸â†’oe`, `ĂĄâ†’aa`). Eksempler: `"Yin og Yang"` â†’ `yin-og-yang`, `"ForĂ¦ldrelĂ¸se + ForĂ¦ldede"` â†’ `foraeldreloese-foraeldede`, `"Christian Broberg"` â†’ `christian-broberg`. Slugs er en Neurons kanoniske adresse inden i en Trail â€” filen er `<slug>.md`, URL'en er `/kb/<kb-slug>/neurons/<slug>`, og et Neuron-links `[[...]]`-tekst slugifiseres for at finde sit mĂĄl. To forskellige titler der slugifiseres til samme streng kolliderer pĂĄ samme Neuron, sĂĄ kompileren undgĂĄr flertydighed ved at holde titler distinkte i kildesproget. Den dansk-engelske bilingual-fold (F148, `normalizedSlug`) genkender at `yin-og-yang` og `yin-and-yang` skal ramme samme Neuron nĂĄr Trail'ens sprog er dansk â€” beskytter mod LLM-drift mellem `og` / `and`.

definition.en: The inverse of a Neuron-link. If Neuron A contains [[B]], then B has a backlink FROM A. Shown on the Neuron reader as "N Neurons link here" â€” lets the Curator see how interconnected a Neuron is.
definition.da: Det omvendte af et Neuron-link. Hvis Neuron A indeholder [[B]], sĂĄ har B et baglink FRA A. Vist pĂĄ Neuron-lĂ¦seren som "N Neuroner linker hertil" â€” lader Kuratoren se hvor forbundet en Neuron er.

definition.en: A Neuron-link that carries a named semantic relation, so the graph isn't just "A links to B" but "A *is-a* B" or "A *contradicts* B". Authored as `[[target|edge-type]]`. Seven recognised types (F137, `wiki_backlinks.edge_type`): **cites** â€” bare mention, weakest relation, the default for every plain `[[link]]` and every pre-F137 row; **is-a** â€” hierarchical specialisation (Labrador is-a Dog); **part-of** â€” composition (Keyboard part-of Laptop); **contradicts** â€” an explicit disagreement between two claims, feeds the contradiction-lint detector directly; **supersedes** â€” versioning (Constitution-1953 supersedes Constitution-1849); **example-of** â€” a concrete instance of an abstract concept (Pythagoras example-of Mathematician); **caused-by** â€” causal dependency (Flood caused-by Dam-break). The compile-prompt teaches the LLM when each type applies; the backlink extractor records the type in the DB on save; the Graph view colours edges by type with a legend-chip row, and the relationships API exposes a `?type=` filter for targeted queries ("show me everything this Neuron contradicts"). Turning a bare `[[link]]` into a typed one is a drop-in edit â€” no schema migration, the default stays `cites` for anything un-typed.
definition.da: Et Neuron-link der bĂ¦rer en navngiven semantisk relation, sĂĄ grafen ikke bare siger "A linker til B" men "A *is-a* B" eller "A *modsiger* B". Skrives som `[[mĂĄl|edge-type]]`. Syv genkendte typer (F137, `wiki_backlinks.edge_type`): **cites** â€” simpel omtale, svageste relation, standard for ethvert bart `[[link]]` og alle rĂ¦kker fra fĂ¸r F137; **is-a** â€” hierarkisk specialisering (Labrador is-a Hund); **part-of** â€” komposition (Tastatur part-of Laptop); **contradicts** â€” eksplicit uenighed mellem to pĂĄstande, fĂ¸der contradiction-lint-detektoren direkte; **supersedes** â€” versionering (Grundloven-1953 supersedes Grundloven-1849); **example-of** â€” et konkret eksempel pĂĄ et abstrakt koncept (Pythagoras example-of Matematiker); **caused-by** â€” kausal afhĂ¦ngighed (OversvĂ¸mmelse caused-by DĂ¦mningsbrud). Kompilerings-prompten lĂ¦rer LLM'en hvornĂĄr hver type gĂ¦lder; baglink-extractoren registrerer typen i DB'en ved gemning; Graf-visningen farver kanter efter type med en legend-chip-rĂ¦kke, og relationships-API'et har en `?type=`-filter til mĂĄlrettede forespĂ¸rgsler ("vis mig alt denne Neuron modsiger"). At forvandle et bart `[[link]]` til et typet er en drop-in-Ă¦ndring â€” ingen schema-migration, standarden forbliver `cites` for alt uden type.

definition.en: The engine-level operation behind an Action. Examples: approve (commit the candidate's content), reject (discard), retire-neuron (archive a specific page), flag-source (mark a Source as untrustworthy), acknowledge (mark as handled manually). Multiple Actions can share an effect â€” a contradiction's "Retire A" and "Retire B" both use retire-neuron with different documentId args.
definition.da: Motor-niveau-operationen bag en Handling. Eksempler: godkend (commit kandidatens indhold), afvis (kasser), arkiver-neuron (arkiver en specifik side), markĂ©r-kilde (markĂ©r en Kilde som upĂĄlidelig), anerkend (markĂ©r som hĂĄndteret manuelt). Flere Handlinger kan dele en effekt â€” en modsigelses "Arkiver A" og "Arkiver B" bruger begge arkiver-neuron med forskellige documentId-argumenter.

definition.en: A 0-1 number on every machine-generated Candidate representing how sure the pipeline is. High confidence (â‰Ą0.8 by default) can auto-approve via policy; lower confidence lands pending for Curator review. Human-submitted candidates always land pending regardless.
definition.da: Et 0-1 tal pĂĄ hver maskine-genereret Kandidat der reprĂ¦senterer hvor sikker pipelinen er. HĂ¸j sikkerhed (â‰Ą0,8 som standard) kan auto-godkendes via politik; lavere sikkerhed lander afventende til Kurator-gennemgang. Menneske-indsendte kandidater lander altid afventende uanset.

definition.en: A one-click action on an Orphan Neuron finding. The LLM reads the Neuron's content, reviews the top-matching Sources in the same Trail (pre-filtered by keyword overlap), and proposes the 1â€“3 Sources the Neuron's claims most likely came from. If confident, it patches the Neuron's frontmatter `sources: [...]` and the orphan alert disappears. If nothing matches clearly, it asks the Curator to link manually. Uses Claude Haiku to keep the cost predictable.
definition.da: Ă‰t-klik-handling pĂĄ et ForĂ¦ldrelĂ¸s-Neuron-fund. LLM'en lĂ¦ser Neuronens indhold, gennemgĂĄr de tĂ¦ttest matchende Kilder i samme Trail (forhĂĄndsfiltreret pĂĄ nĂ¸gleords-overlap) og foreslĂĄr de 1-3 Kilder som Neuronens pĂĄstande mest sandsynligt kom fra. Er den sikker, patcher den Neuronens frontmatter `sources: [...]` og forĂ¦ldrelĂ¸s-advarslen forsvinder. Finder den ikke et tydeligt match, beder den Kuratoren linke manuelt. Bruger Claude Haiku for forudsigelige omkostninger.

definition.en: The primary "yes, commit this" action on a Candidate. Triggers the effect behind the Action (create Neuron, update Neuron, archive, etc.) and logs a wiki_event with the Curator as actor. Every Candidate has at least one approve-effect action; contradiction and orphan findings replace it with more specific affirmative actions like Reconcile or Auto-link sources.
definition.da: Den primĂ¦re "ja, udfĂ¸r dette"-handling pĂĄ en Kandidat. UdlĂ¸ser effekten bag Handlingen (opret Neuron, opdatĂ©r Neuron, arkivĂ©r, osv.) og logger et wiki_event med Kuratoren som afsender. Hver Kandidat har mindst Ă©n godkend-effekt-handling; modsigelses- og forĂ¦ldrelĂ¸s-fund erstatter den med mere specifikke bekrĂ¦ftende handlinger som Forlig eller Auto-link kilder.

definition.en: Discard a Candidate without committing its change. Used when a lint finding is a false alarm, a chat-answer turned out to be wrong, or the Curator disagrees with a compiler-proposed update. Leaves the target Neuron unchanged. Under the default Trusting Lint Policy, dismissed lint findings don't re-fire â€” under Strict, they may re-qualify on the next run.
definition.da: Kasser en Kandidat uden at commit'e dens Ă¦ndring. Bruges nĂĄr et lint-fund er falsk alarm, et chat-svar viste sig forkert, eller Kuratoren er uenig med en compiler-foreslĂĄet opdatering. Lader mĂĄl-Neuronen vĂ¦re uĂ¦ndret. Under standard Tillidsfuld Lint-politik dukker afviste lint-fund ikke op igen â€” under Streng kan de kvalificere sig igen ved nĂ¦ste kĂ¸rsel.

definition.en: Soft-delete a Neuron or Source. The document stays in the database (its data, metadata, and history are preserved) but disappears from the active lists. Reversible at any time from the Archived tab. Different from hard-deletion: Archive preserves the audit trail, links pointing to the archived Neuron become broken links until cleaned up, and the file on disk stays intact for Sources.
definition.da: BlĂ¸d-sletning af en Neuron eller Kilde. Dokumentet bliver i databasen (dets data, metadata og historik bevares), men forsvinder fra de aktive lister. Kan altid gendannes fra Arkiveret-fanen. Forskellig fra hĂĄrd sletning: ArkivĂ©r bevarer audit-sporet, links der peger pĂĄ den arkiverede Neuron bliver brudte links indtil de ryddes op, og selve filen pĂĄ disken forbliver intakt for Kilders vedkommende.

definition.en: A contradiction-specific variant of Archive. When two Neurons contradict each other, the Curator picks which one to retire â€” effectively the same action as Archive, but scoped to the contradiction resolution so the audit trail records WHY the Neuron was taken out of circulation. Canonical Danish translation: ArkivĂ©r (not "PensionĂ©r" â€” that sounds like a person retiring from work, misleading here).
definition.da: En modsigelses-specifik variant af ArkivĂ©r. NĂĄr to Neuroner modsiger hinanden, vĂ¦lger Kuratoren hvilken der skal arkiveres â€” i praksis samme handling som ArkivĂ©r, men scopet til modsigelsens afvikling sĂĄ audit-sporet dokumenterer HVORFOR Neuronen blev taget ud af cirkulation. Korrekt dansk oversĂ¦ttelse: ArkivĂ©r (ikke "PensionĂ©r" â€” det lyder som et menneske der gĂĄr pĂĄ pension, misvisende her).

definition.en: A contradiction-resolution path that doesn't archive either Neuron. The Curator opens both in the editor and writes a new, merged Neuron that honors both sources' claims â€” e.g. "under condition X, A is true; under condition Y, B is true." Both original Neurons then get Archive'd or rewritten to reference the reconciled version. Canonical Danish: Forlig (not "Afstem" or "Forene" â€” those suggest voting or simple concatenation, which this isn't).
definition.da: En modsigelses-afklaring der ikke arkiverer nogen af Neuronerne. Kuratoren ĂĄbner begge i editoren og skriver en ny, sammensmeltet Neuron der respekterer begge kilders pĂĄstande â€” fx "under betingelse X er A sand; under betingelse Y er B sand." Begge oprindelige Neuroner bliver derefter arkiveret eller omskrevet til at referere til den samlede version. Korrekt dansk: Forlig (ikke "Afstem" eller "Forene" â€” de antyder afstemning eller simpel sammenfĂ¸jning, hvilket det ikke er).

definition.en: Large Language Model â€” the AI that does the heavy lifting inside Trail. Two specific jobs: (1) Compile Neurons from Sources during ingest (read the Source, extract concepts + claims, write structured markdown with provenance); (2) Lint Neurons for contradictions, propose orphanâ†’Source links, translate action labels between EN/DA. Trail runs the LLM via the local Claude Code CLI â€” no Anthropic API key required, Max subscription billing. The Curator is always the final authority: the LLM proposes, the Curator disposes.
definition.da: Large Language Model â€” den AI der gĂ¸r det tunge arbejde i Trail. To specifikke opgaver: (1) KompilĂ©r Neuroner fra Kilder under ingest (lĂ¦s Kilden, udtrĂ¦k koncepter + pĂĄstande, skriv struktureret markdown med herkomst); (2) Lint Neuroner for modsigelser, foreslĂĄ forĂ¦ldrelĂ¸sâ†’Kilde-links, oversĂ¦t handlings-labels mellem EN/DA. Trail kĂ¸rer LLM'en via den lokale Claude Code CLI â€” ingen Anthropic API-nĂ¸gle krĂ¦vet, Max-abonnements-afregning. Kuratoren er altid den endelige autoritet: LLM'en foreslĂĄr, Kuratoren beslutter.
